name: Three-Way Shortbreads
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the flour and sugar.
2. Cut in butter until mixture resembles fine crumbs.
3. Form mixture into a ball and knead until smooth.
Shortbread Wedges:
4. On an ungreased cookie sheet, pat or roll dough into an 8-inch circle.
5. For a scalloped edge, use your forefinger and thumb to create indentations around the edge of the dough circle.
6. Using a sharp knife, cut the dough circle into 12-16 pie-shaped wedges.
7. Do not separate the wedges.
8. Prick each wedge with a fork to form a design.
9. Bake at 325°F oven for 25-30 minutes or until edges are light brown. While warm, re-cut wedges.
10. Remove from pan and cool on a wire rack.
Thumbprint Cookies:
11. Prepare dough as directed; shape dough into 1-inch balls.
12. Place balls 2 inches apart on an ungreased cookie sheet.
13. Using your thumb, press an indentation in the center of each cookie.
14. Bake at 325°F oven for about 18 minutes or until bottoms are light brown and cookies are slightly golden.
15. Immediately spoon 1/2 teaspoon fruit preserves (such as cherry, strawberry, or peach) into each indentation.
16. Cool completely, then remove cookies from the cookie sheet.
Pecan Spice Shortbread:
17. Prepare dough as directed, except substitute brown sugar for the sugar and add 1 teaspoon apple pie spice to the flour mixture.
18. Pat the dough into an ungreased 9-by-9-by-2-inch baking pan.
19. Sprinkle dough with 1/4 cup chopped pecans; press nuts lightly into dough.
20. Bake at 325°F oven for 23-25 minutes.
21. While warm, cut into 16 1/4-inch squares.
22. Cut each square diagonally to make triangles.
23. Cool in pan and drizzle with vanilla icing.
Vanilla Icing:
24. In a small mixing bowl, combine 1/2 cup sifted powdered sugar and 1/4 teaspoon vanilla extract.
25. Stir in enough milk (2-3 teaspoons) to make an icing of drizzling consistency.
